days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: First taste of Japan's vibrant culture and cuisine

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Arrive at Narita International Airport, go through customs, and pick up your luggage. Head to the station for transportation to the city center.

timeline.title: Check in to Shinjuku Granbell Hotel
timeline.detail: Settle into your hotel, which is only a 10-minute walk from Shinjuku Station. Enjoy the modern amenities and breathtaking city views.

timeline.title: Explore Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely stroll through Shinjuku Gyoen, a beautiful oasis amidst the city's skyscrapers. The cherry blossoms might still be stunning in spring.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner at Omoide Yokocho
timeline.detail: Savor traditional yakitori and other Japanese izakaya dishes at the famous Omoide Yokocho alley. Experience the vibrant atmosphere as you eat among locals.

timeline.title: Shibuya Crossing Experience
timeline.detail: Visit the iconic Shibuya Crossing, the busiest pedestrian crossing in the world. Capture the frenetic energy of the city as you navigate through the crowd.

timeline.title: Nightcap at an Izakaya in Shibuya
timeline.detail: End the night with drinks at a local izakaya in Shibuya. Sample a variety of Japanese sake and beers while mingling with other travelers.

localTips: Purchase a Suica card at the airport for easy subway travel around Tokyo.
Be cautious of taxi fares; they can be expensive compared to trains.
If you're planning to visit more than 2 attractions, consider a Tokyo sightseeing pass for discounts.
Learn a few basic Japanese phrases; locals appreciate the effort!
Explore the small alleys of Shinjuku for hidden food gems.

days.title: Cultural Day in Asakusa and Ueno
days.subtitle: Discover traditional Tokyo

timeline.title: Breakfast at Suke6 Diner
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty breakfast of pancakes and fresh juices at this vibrant local diner in the heart of Asakusa, known for its unique decor.

timeline.title: Visit Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Explore the iconic Senso-ji Temple, Tokyo's oldest temple, along with its vibrant Nakamise shopping street, offering a variety of snacks and souvenirs.

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Okonomiyaki Saito
timeline.detail: Try authentic okonomiyaki made to order at Asakusa Okonomiyaki Saito. Customize with your favorite ingredients and enjoy at this local eatery.

timeline.title: Explore Ueno Park
timeline.detail: Visit Ueno Park, home to museums, a zoo, and beautiful cherry blossoms. Spend the afternoon wandering the park and its surrounding attractions.

timeline.title: Dinner at Ameyoko Market
timeline.detail: Dine at Ameyoko Market, a bustling street market with many eateries. Sample street food such as takoyaki and grilled fish while exploring the vibrant stalls.

timeline.title: Evening Drinks in Ueno
timeline.detail: Relax with a drink at a local bar in Ueno, reflecting on the day. Chat with locals or fellow travelers while enjoying a casual atmosphere.

localTips: Try to swap yen in your home country to avoid higher exchange rates.
For a fun local experience, consider renting a kimono for pictures around Senso-ji.
Bargain at Ameyoko market for souvenirs and snacks.
Respect local customs at religious sites, such as bowing at Senso-ji.
Public transportation is the best way to navigate the city quickly.

days.title: Shopping and Departure
days.subtitle: Last-minute shopping in Tokyo

timeline.title: Breakfast at Tsukiji Outer Market
timeline.detail: Start your day early in Tsukiji Outer Market with fresh sushi and seafood. Experience the lively market atmosphere while enjoying your breakfast.

timeline.title: Shopping in Harajuku
timeline.detail: Head to Harajuku and explore the trendy shops on Takeshita Street. Don't miss the chance to find unique fashion and quirky souvenirs.

timeline.title: Lunch at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Indulge in a solo dining experience at Ichiran, famous for its tonkotsu ramen. Enjoy each bowl in a private booth for a unique taste.

timeline.title: Head to Narita Airport
timeline.detail: Check out from your hotel and take the Narita Express from Shinjuku to the airport. Ensure you have enough time for anything last-minute at the airport.

timeline.title: Depart from Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Head to your departure gate and prepare for boarding. Reflect on the memorable experiences you've had in Tokyo as you bid farewell to this vibrant city.

localTips: Check your flight times and gate before heading to the airport.
Look for duty-free shops in Narita for last-minute gifts.
Public transportation is the best option to get to the airport.
Keep some yen in cash, as smaller shops may not accept cards.
Stay connected with a portable Wi-Fi device, especially for maps.

hotels.name: Shinjuku Granbell Hotel
hotels.note: Modern design, 10-min walk from Shinjuku Station.
hotels.address: 2 Chome-14-5 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo, 160-0021, Japan

budget.note: Each Suica card costs ¥2,000 with ¥1,500 for transport
